raw_text: nestor isaac garcia rueda address: calle javier garcía de leániz, 46 torrelodones, madrid, 28250 telephone: +3491859 6155 mobile: +34625208811 e-mail: nestorgarciarueda@outlook.com nationality: spain academic education 2013 - 2017 degree and master in electronic & electrical engineering (meng in electronic & electrical engineering) at university of leeds (top russel group university), leeds, united kingdom. final individual project: the wireless 360 vr driving vehicle. project awarded with: 71%. 2011-2013 btec extended diploma in engineering hugh baird college, liverpool, united kingdom. awarded with triple maximum grade distinction* distinction* distinction*. 2007 - 2011 obligatory secondary education (o.s.e) instituto de educación secundaria (1.e.s) diego velázquez. torrelodones, madrid, spain. nov 2017- may 2018 monitoring engineer alstom alstom. madrid, spain. completed 6 months development and innovation project for the ave train fleet s100t and s100f, consisting of thermal heat monitoring, of electronic component overheats towards the prevention of risks and fires that may affect the passengers safety, drivers or personnel on- board. programming language implemented for development: c++, unix. environment hardware system: sbc (single-board computer) raspberry pi 3. also worked with gcc, crontab, shell, bash and qt. contributor of the simulator design and implementation of the new software version for s.i.a.c, integrated in the ave train on-board systems, through the reverse engineering process methodology. programming language implemented for development: python. elaborated data management in rstudio with implementation and filtering techniques, for avoiding empty corrupted files in alstom database, while administrating data transferences between temporary to permanent directories in the alstom cloud server. programming language: r. performed installations, and maintenance of remote electronic monitoring boxes, for carrying performance analysis readings of on-board systems mtus (motor traction units) of the ave s100 fleet. may 2018 june 2018 banking services programming engineer world line worldline iberia s.a. madrid, spain. e-payment services card validation processes, e-payment services implementations programming, activation and de-activation of credit cards, format field check rules of fixed and variable lengths. programming language implemented for development: c++, linux cli. programming environment: linux, docker, git and eclipse. engineering skills final year master degree individual project: the wireless 360 vr driving vehicle involved in the automation of the robot vehicle chassis (rp5-cho2), use of control systems for the direction and execution intensity of the electric motors (dc brushed), "h-bridge for its piloting driving pulses control. as well as the end-to-end protection of the telecommunication system, for preventing from unwanted wireless hijacking for its restricted driving, by radio frequency signal encryption (transmitting over ism rf 434mhz). remote virtual reality driving recreation through a stereoscopic helmet display modification, 3d and automatic 360 vision capability for the vehicle driving experience (x2 pi cameras v.2), through uniaxial head movements, mimicking and detection system implementation techniques, used for the automatic angular rotational vision mode, matching the driver user rotation gestures. programming gyroscope itg3200 sensor axes control (itg3200) on the x and y axes (pitch and yaw 16-bits 400khz), data compensation and conversions, implementation and programming in raspberry pi 3 model b (python), as well as communication protocol master-slave (12c) for mbed nxp lpc1768 microcontroller in c++, and servo 360 rotational motors, project aimed for military rescue and defence missions. final year degree group project: the autonomous smart vehicle, hall-effect sensors programing development in c++ (ss495a) towards the detection of magnetic field concentration events, used in the approach of triangulation of magnetizing field overflows, and the implementation for speed performance and monitoring tracking control, of the wheels of the vehicle, using the communication protocol can bus. interaction exchange through a single cable of medium, with other microcontrollers or sbc of the team, used for data control operations and communication interactions, with a transceivers chip (mcp2551), regulating voltage conversion levels for digital binary speech, with a differential transmission at 1mb/s. implemented coordination towards obtaining with greater accuracy, the vehicle system surrounding distances in the environment, with high-precision ultrasonic guidance sensors srfo5, with pwm echo and trigger acoustic signals propagations, towards vehicle autonomous guidance, with logic gate simplifying operability for two coordinated common grouped triggering ultrasound arranged events (multiplex system approach), to accommodate eight sensors, functionality at two tempos, reducing and narrowing aliasing interference effects, in operability state. data sensors contribution towards vehicle driving a.i. experienced with cad pcb designing software eagle, used for the hardware development, of manufacturing layout designs. skilful in cad simulation programmes such as: ads, multisim, coolterm, teraterm, mbed, xcode (apple applications development platform), wireshark and matlab. programmer and developer of the individual project the qapp for iphone 5s devices (using objective c-class oriented language). developer of the ultrasound obstacle detection & parking assistance system (2nd year degree). hardware + software design in c++. experienced in verilog and vhdl programming languages for fpga, python and linux. group project manager for the "traxster project (university), through wireless communication (end-to-end) rf mo-sawr-a/mo-rx3400-a and data protection under signal encoders and decoders (ht12e/ht12d). ability to produce 2d designs, according to the british regulation british standard bs8888. software developed the game of life project using c++ programming language in mbed hardware microcontroller, tested with nokia n5510 lcd screen. experienced dealing with defective electronic devices, maintenance of consumer units and resolution to hardware and software issues. + implementation of static ip assignation on linux, based on dhcp protocol. familiar with using bsn (body sensor networks), and electrical installation circuits ring main, for the wiring of light bulbs, plugs, switches and node connections. + abilities to design electrical and electronic circuits and soldering electronic components. personal skills proficient in microsoft excel, word y power point. + resourceful administrative, installing, formatting, flashing and defragmenting operative systems (o.s) of computers from both apple and windows 10/8/7/vista/xp...etc. + experienced in personal services for the replacement of mobile screens, fix and solder contacts in hardware failures as well as in physical buttons, headphone, batteries and housing modifications in smartphones and chassis. + highly dedicated and mature. lived for more than 6 years in england, since was 17, for studying reasons 2011-2017. + enthusiastic, responsible, organized and hard worker. + ability to work individually, as well as in a group. also to lead. + useful interpretation and translation skills to act as bridge of international communication between spanish and english. languages spanish - native. english - ielts, fce, ket, pet and esol (english second official language). honor awards & memberships 2016 honoured with the prestigious riley award 2016 prize, awarded by the leeds university union (luu), in recognition of excellence and commitment to the electronic & electrical engineering department society. 2015-2016 elected voted member for the position welfare officer for the engineering department shocksock society. contributor to the leadership, organization and management. 2013-2014 society member and contributor of the engineers without borders (ewb). 2013 member of the institution of engineering : technology (iet). affiliate membership number: 1100344903. academic references dr a.h.kemps, academic and supervisor on my last year module project the 360 driving experience, senior lecturer in the areas of communication networking, routing & radio communications, at university of leeds. e-mail: a.h.kemp @leeds.ac.uk dr r.clarke, director of student education at the university of leeds, academic in the field of high frequency circuits & measurements, personal supervisor in the group project the autonomous smart vehicle and contributor of the book metrology for vector network analyzers. e-mail: r.g.clarke@leeds.ac.uk
